page: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Dpdsv6 server family. It is powered by a Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz, featuring 48 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core. The hardware configuration includes 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the server comes equipped with a bundled 2835 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage. It does not include any GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory subsystem performance. While single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the average category, multi-core performance is highly competitive, with Geekbench multi-core scores reaching the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth is a notable strength, with uncached large-block read and write speeds both ranking in the top 10% tier. Additionally, Passmark database operations and memory scores are positioned in the top 10% tier, while Redis SET operations and static web serving throughput demonstrate strong top 25% performance.

The server exhibits specific performance tradeoffs, particularly in low-complexity or single-threaded tasks. For instance, LLM text generation for small models and prompt processing for large models are categorized as weak. However, the high physical core density and exceptional memory bandwidth make this instance highly cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads that do not require hyperthreading. The architectural profile and benchmark results make the D48pds_v6 highly suitable for database operations, multi-threaded web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
description: The Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is an ARM-based general-purpose server powered by a 48-core Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor running at 3.4 GHz. Featuring 192.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 2835 GB local disk, this instance delivers a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Benchmark results highlight exceptional memory subsystem performance, with uncached large-block read and write bandwidth and Passmark database operations ranking in the top 10% tier. Multi-core execution is highly efficient, though single-core performance and certain LLM inference tasks represent performance tradeoffs. Lacking GPU acceleration, the server is qualitatively cost-efficient for highly parallelized, memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for database management, static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is an ARM64 server with 48 Neoverse-N2 cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance for multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D48pds_v6 is an ARM64 server featuring 48 physical cores, 192 GB RAM, and 2835 GB local storage for database and general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An ARM-powered general-purpose instance featuring forty-eight physical cores, massive local storage, and top-tier memory bandwidth for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 48-core Microsoft ARM Neoverse-N2 processor at 3.4 GHz
Equipped with 192.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 2835 GB local disk
Top-tier memory bandwidth and database operation benchmark performance
Dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading for predictable execution
Well-suited for database hosting, web serving, and software compilation
